preview_text:
The model favors Uruguay with a 65% win probability, while Saudi Arabia is given a 10.9% chance to prevail. The gap of roughly 54 percentage points reflects a heavily skewed matchup in which the underdog would require a significant upset to claim three points.

The draw emerges as the second-most-likely outcome at 24.1%, suggesting that even though Uruguay enters as a clear favorite, the match carries meaningful variance. The win-probability spread indicates competitive imbalance rather than a blowout certainty; a stalemate remains plausible, and Saudi Arabia's chances, though modest, are not negligible.

The sportsbook consensus and Polymarket prices track the model closely. Uruguay's win probability stands at 65.6% and 66.2% respectively, while draw estimates settle around 22%, creating alignment within 2 percentage points across all three sources. This convergence suggests little disagreement on the fundamental match structure.
